proved: ProviderTaskRequest has no disclosure-accepted field, so a webview caller cannot assert its own human approval.
The registered provider_generate command validates bounded model/category disclosure data and uses a Rust-owned native dialog with explicit Send once and Cancel buttons.
The dialog call runs off the main thread, and only its boolean result becomes the one-use transport approval.
A denied call returns a content-free failed provider-run record before credential lookup, cancellation registration, or network transport; the denial test uses a vault that panics if read.
The disclosure copy names provider, model, exact destination, content categories, one request, storage request, zero-retention status, and dated policy source without embedding raw prompt content.
Model names, content categories, task identifiers, generation bounds, and cancellation identifiers fail closed before they can mislead the dialog or enter runtime state.
Generation, cancellation, and credential operations are registered only through typed tauri.ts wrappers.
The compiled MCP remains at twelve tools, has no provider-generation or credential authority, reports the new limited status, and keeps stdout protocol-clean.
The Rust-authored conformance record still passes the public JSON schema and TypeScript semantic validator without credential or prompt canaries.
notProved: A human clicked the real packaged OS dialog; that requires a GUI smoke check.
A product settings or research workflow UI can configure and start the call; no product component calls the typed commands yet.
Caller-described categories match a frozen domain task; category derivation and input-hash binding remain a workflow gate.
A live OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or xAI request, current policy behavior, billing, or retention attestation.
Streaming, tools, retries, reasoning continuity, or adversarial multi-provider orchestration.
Transient key-entry DOM/memory, macOS/Linux credential stores, logs, exports, or crash artifacts are leak-free.
